meta_title: Impact Doors in Century Village, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Century Village, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Call for a free quote today!
h1: Impact Doors for Century Village Homes — Real Hurricane Protection That Lasts
intro:
Century Village in West Palm Beach is one of Palm Beach County's most beloved active-adult communities — a tight-knit neighborhood of condominium-style homes, manicured grounds, and a lifestyle built around comfort and community. But underneath all that charm lies a critical vulnerability that too many homeowners in the 33409 ZIP code haven't yet addressed: aging entry points that simply weren't built to survive today's hurricane seasons. With approximately 80% of homes in the area predating 1994 — the year Florida's modern building codes were fundamentally reshaped — the vast majority of Century Village residences are still protected by standard doors that offer little more than symbolic resistance against serious storm forces.

Palm Beach County sits in a 140 mph design wind zone, meaning every door protecting your home must be engineered and installed to withstand that level of sustained wind pressure. When you factor in the community's history with storms like Hurricane Frances in 2004, Hurricane Wilma in 2005, and Hurricane Irma in 2017 — seven named storms impacting the area since 1990 — the urgency becomes clear. Century Village is located roughly 8 miles from the Atlantic coastline, which places it directly in the path of storm surge-driven wind bands and intense pressure differentials that can catastrophically compromise standard doors at their weakest points: the frame, the locking mechanism, and the glass panel.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has helped hundreds of homeowners throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties upgrade to impact-rated protection, and we understand what matters most to Century Village residents. With average home values around $160,000, every improvement decision has to make smart financial sense. The good news is that impact doors don't just protect — they add real value, reduce energy costs, qualify for significant homeowner's insurance discounts, and can be financed with as little as $0 down starting at $87 per month. For Century Village homeowners, that's a meaningful upgrade that pays for itself over time.

section_why.h2: Why Century Village Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Door Installation
section_why.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've earned the nickname 'The Kings of Service' by doing things the way they should be done — with experienced in-house crews, premium materials, and zero subcontracting. That last point matters more than many homeowners realize. When a company sends a subcontracted crew to install your impact doors, accountability gets blurry fast. Who do you call if there's a problem with the frame seal six months later? With Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, the answer is simple: you call us, and we show up. Every installation in Century Village is performed by our own licensed and insured technicians, backed by over 60 years of combined experience across our team.

We've worked throughout the Century Village West Palm Beach community and understand the specific challenges these homes present. Many units were built in the 1970s and 1980s with non-standard rough openings, older masonry construction, and framing conditions that require experienced hands to properly address before a new impact door can be safely set. Our pre-installation assessments identify these conditions before the work begins, not after — which is how we've successfully completed upgrades in 500+ South Florida homes without the surprises that other contractors often leave behind.

Our product lineup for Century Village includes impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors, all sourced from industry-trusted brands ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. These are not off-the-shelf big-box products. They are purpose-built hurricane protection systems tested to meet and exceed Florida Product Approval standards. From the multi-point locking systems to the laminated glass cores, every component is selected for performance under real storm conditions.

We also make the permitting process completely hands-off for you. Our team manages all documentation, submits to the Palm Beach County Building Division on your behalf, and coordinates the required inspections from start to finish. You don't need to navigate the county permit portal or chase down an inspector — we handle it all, so your project stays on schedule and fully compliant.

section_history.h2: A Storm History That Proves Century Village Needs Better Door Protection
section_history.body:
Century Village has weathered more than its share of serious storms. Hurricane Frances made landfall along the Treasure Coast in September 2004, bringing sustained winds and prolonged rainfall that lasted nearly 18 hours over Palm Beach County — one of the longest-lasting hurricane impacts the region had seen in decades. Just over a year later, Hurricane Wilma tore across South Florida in October 2005 with a forward speed that generated extreme pressure changes, shattering windows and destroying doors throughout the county in a matter of minutes. Wilma remains one of the costliest storms in Palm Beach County history, and Century Village residents who had not yet upgraded experienced firsthand how quickly an unprotected entry door can fail.

Hurricane Irma's arrival in September 2017 served as the region's most recent major reminder. While the Palm Beach area was spared a direct hit, the sustained tropical-force winds and feeder bands that struck the 33409 corridor were enough to compromise aging door frames, blow out standard sliding glass doors, and generate interior pressure failures in homes that lacked proper protection. Seven named storms affecting this community since 1990 is not an abstract statistic — it represents seven separate opportunities for unprotected doors to fail, for water intrusion to destroy interiors, and for homeowners to be left without safe shelter during a crisis.

The lesson from Century Village's storm history is not that the threat is occasional — it's that the threat is recurring. Upgrading to impact-rated doors with certified wind resistance means you're not gambling on whether this season's storm will be the one that finally exposes your home's weakest entry points.

section_code.h2: Palm Beach County Building Codes and What They Mean for Your Impact Door Upgrade
section_code.body:
Century Village falls under Palm Beach County's jurisdiction for all building permits and inspections, and while the area is not classified as High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation reserved primarily for Miami-Dade and Broward coastal areas — this does not mean standard doors are acceptable. Florida Building Code still mandates that all exterior door replacements in Palm Beach County meet hurricane-rated product standards appropriate for the area's 140 mph design wind speed. Any door installed without proper Florida Product Approval is a code violation that can result in failed inspections, insurance claim denials, and significant out-of-pocket liability during a storm event.

The Palm Beach County Building Division requires a permit for any exterior door replacement, including impact door upgrades. This permit process involves submitting the Florida Product Approval number for the door system, the manufacturer's installation specifications, and the contractor's license information before work begins. Once installation is complete, a county inspector must verify that the door was set properly, that the frame was anchored according to the approved drawings, and that the opening meets current energy efficiency requirements under the Florida Energy Conservation Code.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this entire process for Century Village clients. We maintain active relationships with the Palm Beach County Building Division, submit complete permit packages upfront to minimize delays, and ensure every installation passes its final inspection the first time. For homes built before 1994 — which represents roughly 80% of Century Village — we also conduct a thorough assessment of the existing rough opening and substrate to confirm the installation will meet current structural anchoring requirements. Cutting corners on permitting might seem like a way to save time, but it can void your manufacturer's warranty, disqualify your insurance wind mitigation credit, and create serious problems if you ever try to sell your home.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Built to Protect Century Village Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two of the industry's most respected manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are Florida Product Approved, third-party tested, and engineered specifically for the demanding conditions of South Florida's hurricane environment. For Century Village homeowners, these are not cosmetic upgrades — they are structural safety systems that have been rigorously tested to resist not just wind pressure but also the windborne debris impact that accompanies high-speed storms.

ESW's impact door lineup includes heavy-duty entry doors, French doors, and sliding glass door systems featuring reinforced aluminum frames and laminated safety glass cores. The laminated glass is the critical element — bonded with a polymer interlayer, it holds together even when shattered by impact, preventing the dangerous pressure equalization that occurs when a door or window fails during a hurricane. ESW doors also feature multi-point locking systems that engage at multiple points along the door frame simultaneously, distributing load evenly and dramatically reducing the risk of blow-in at the threshold, the header, or the side jambs.

CWI impact doors offer a complementary lineup with an emphasis on aesthetic versatility and thermal performance. For Century Village homeowners who want impact protection without sacrificing the look of their home, CWI's range of panel designs, finish options, and glass configurations allows for a customized result that fits the community's architectural character. Both ESW and CWI products carry the wind resistance ratings required for Palm Beach County's 140 mph wind zone, and both are fully compatible with the multi-point hardware and frame anchoring systems required under current Florida Building Code. Our team will help you select the right product for your specific opening, budget, and aesthetic goals.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Century Village — and How to Make Them Affordable
section_cost.body:
For Century Village homeowners managing budgets carefully in a community with an average home value of around $160,000, the cost of impact door installation is an understandably important consideration. The reality is that impact door pricing varies based on door type, size, configuration, and the condition of the existing opening. Entry door replacements typically fall in a different range than sliding glass door systems, and French door configurations with sidelights or transoms carry their own pricing. What stays consistent is that Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers transparent, itemized quotes with no hidden fees — and financing options designed to make the upgrade accessible without financial strain.

Through our financing partners, Century Village homeowners can get started with $0 down and monthly payments from as low as $87 per month, depending on the project scope and approved terms. This means a full impact entry door upgrade can often be completed for less per month than the cost of a streaming service bundle and a couple of restaurant meals. More importantly, the return on that monthly investment begins immediately — in the form of lower energy bills from improved insulation, reduced homeowner's insurance premiums, and the elimination of costly accordion shutter deployment before every tropical system.

From a pure financial standpoint, impact doors also protect the value of your home. In a community like Century Village, where resale activity is steady and buyers are increasingly savvy about storm protection features, impact doors are a documented selling point. Appraisers recognize hurricane-rated upgrades as value-additive improvements, and buyers looking in the 33409 market often prioritize homes that are already protected. When you combine the monthly savings, the insurance discounts, and the home value impact, the true cost of installing impact doors is considerably lower than the sticker price suggests.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Century Village Homeowners Who Upgrade to Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
One of the most financially impactful benefits of installing impact doors in Century Village is the reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums that typically follows. Florida's insurance market has become one of the most volatile in the country, with carriers routinely non-renewing policies, raising rates, or imposing wind-specific surcharges on homes that lack certified hurricane protection. For Century Village residents — many of whom are on fixed or limited incomes — premium increases can be genuinely disruptive. Impact doors, when combined with a completed wind mitigation inspection, directly address many of the risk factors that insurers cite when calculating premiums.

Florida law requires property insurers to offer premium discounts or credits to policyholders who have installed opening protection that meets specific standards. Impact-rated doors certified to withstand the wind pressures required for Palm Beach County's wind zone qualify for these credits under the Florida Department of Financial Services' wind mitigation framework. The actual discount varies by carrier and policy, but many Century Village homeowners see meaningful annual reductions in their wind portion of the premium — which in Palm Beach County can represent a substantial share of the total policy cost.

To capture these savings, homeowners need a licensed wind mitigation inspector to complete a standardized report verifying the opening protection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing coordinates with local inspectors and ensures that all documentation — Florida Product Approval numbers, installation permits, and inspection sign-offs — is in order before the inspection takes place. We've helped hundreds of South Florida homeowners navigate this process successfully, and we'll make sure Century Village clients have everything they need to maximize their insurance benefit from day one.

faqs.q: Do impact doors really make a difference for a home in Century Village that's only 8 miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Distance from the coast does not meaningfully reduce hurricane wind risk in Palm Beach County. Storms like Frances and Wilma demonstrated that communities well inland from the shoreline experience the same sustained wind speeds that demolish unprotected doors. Century Village's 140 mph wind zone designation reflects this reality. Impact doors provide certified protection against those wind loads and the windborne debris that travels with them — regardless of how far you are from the water.

faqs.q: How much does impact door installation cost in Century Village, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: The cost of impact door installation in Century Village depends on door type, size, and opening conditions, but Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $0 down with monthly payments from as low as $87 per month for qualified buyers. We provide free, no-obligation in-home estimates so you know exactly what your project will cost before committing. Our team can walk you through all available financing options during your consultation.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors with impact doors in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: Yes. The Palm Beach County Building Division requires a permit for all exterior door replacements, including impact upgrades. The permit process involves submitting the door's Florida Product Approval documentation, manufacturer installation specs, and contractor licensing information. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit application and inspection coordination on your behalf, ensuring your project is fully compliant from start to finish — with no headaches for you.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take in Century Village?
faqs.a: Most single-door or multi-door impact installations in Century Village are completed within one to two days once materials are on-site. The full timeline from signed contract to completed installation typically ranges from three to six weeks, depending on permit processing times at the Palm Beach County Building Division and product lead times. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps you informed throughout the process and works to minimize any disruption to your daily routine.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance in Century Village?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation credits to homeowners with certified opening protection like impact doors. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your upgrade, and you submit the report to your insurer to claim the applicable discount. The savings vary by carrier, but many Palm Beach County homeowners see meaningful annual reductions in their wind premium. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing helps ensure all documentation is in place to support your wind mitigation inspection.
